name: Eastern Min
notes: Eastern Min (Mìng-dĕ̤ng-ngṳ̄ / Mindong), including the Fuzhou dialect. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified and NO logographic map is claimed: Chinese characters do not encode pronunciation segmentally, so a character → IPA table is out of scope for this engine. INPUT CONTRACT: as `zh` requires pinyin, a future Eastern Min spec would require romanised input in Bàng-uâ-cê (Foochow Romanized, 平話字) — the syllable-initial / rime / tone-mark spelling — not Han characters. Until such a spec exists this entry carries metadata only. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode mind1253), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode mind1253.

ancestors.notes: Sinitic — the classification node this language sits under.
